result.stdout:
Description: A lightweight C# command-line tool that interacts with the Brave 
API to facilitate web searches from your CLI.

Usage: barvectl [sub-commands] [search-options]

  --help, -h          Print help information.

  Example: barvectl search --filter web --query "where is the ISS" --max 5
  Example: barvectl extract "https://spotthestation.nasa.gov/"

Sub commands:
  search              Search the web for a given search query.
  extract             Convert a given webpage(URL) to text and print to console.

Search options:
  --filter, -f        Result filter. Possible options are videos, web.
  --query, -q         Search query (Max query length 400).
  --country, -c       (Optional: E.g US) Search query country, where the results
come from.
  --lang, -l          (Optional: E.g en) Search language preference.
  --interface, -i     (Optional: E.g en-US) User interface language perferred in
response.
  --safe, -s          (Optional: E.g off) Filter search results for adult 
content. Possible options are off, moderate, strict.
  --max, -m           (Optional: E.g 10) Number of returned result. Default 
count is 5 and max is 20
